reviews.review_msg: Honestly just study for the exams with her detailed study guide and you're good, no cumulative exams. Ample opportunity to earn extra credit, I studied for all my exams less than two days before to get my grade so. Cindy is amazing, so kind and knowledgeable.

She's an amazing teacher. She's incredibly engaging in lectures and communicates the material really well. I also love that she's very clear about what her expectations are for the course and what kind of material will be on tests. She cares a lot about helping students succeed and as long as you work hard in her class, you'll probably do well.

Cindy is a great prof; she breaks the course into unit tests = much easier than mt/final. Be sure to review and commit a lot of the course to memory. If you can review each week and study for the tests youll be just fine. I wouldn't recommend this course as a GPA booster, as the lab exam can be quite a handful (you have to identify 30+ rocks).

50 minutes of lecture and she arrives late every time. Also she doesn't let anyone asks questions in lecture because she's always behind. Cindy has so many problems it's ridiculous, AVOID HER AT ALL COSTS, and tell her to stop wearing that perfume that infects the whole lecture hall with a bad scent! Yuck

Definately NOT a light elective course; lots of memorization of very minor details in slides required. General concepts are interesting but tests are pure memorization of her slides. Participation in everything, even lecture attendance, is graded. Prof cares about student success in her class.

She loves dinosaurs and wants you to succeed. For EASC 103 there were 6 unit tests each worth 15% of your grade, 5% for attendance, 5% for homework. Tests involve a lot of memorizing, but are fair and all multiple choice. Very fair and chill lecturer, lots of memorizing which isn't that bad, definitely worth taking.

She's an amazing lecturer and person, passionate about dinos and learning itself. Homework is just 10-20 mins per lecture, with participation activities and many bonus mark opportunities. She truly wants you to succeed. I got an A+ studying the day before exams, but I would say my memory is probably above average. Take that for what you will.

Best professor you'd ever have should you need to achieve Breadth-Science credit at SFU! 

Her lectures are very interesting, and all you need to do is invest 15+ hours a week when studying for the unit tests!
Cindy is super nice and fun. Though if you struggle with memorization at all this is a class you should 100% avoid.
Cindy is goated. She has very fun lectures, maybe one of the best profs I've had. Supppper heavy on memorization but she's an awesome teacher.

Clearly really cares about her students and is very passionate about the material. The tests are all lecture based and she provides in-depth study materials to help students do well. Would happily take more of her classes

Tests are relatively straightforward (50 MCQ questions). I think the people who rated her low didn't really put in the effort to study the review questions and spend a little bit of time reading the lecture slides. She's very accessible with office hours and she posts lecture recordings if you missed class. 10/10, lovely dino enthusiast.
shes very cool and has lots of dinosaur models. lectures are fun and easy to keep up with. tests are all mc and are based on lecture slides, no additional readings required. rawr
Best university instructor I've ever had!

Amazing prof, very kind and a good lecturer, definitely cares about what she is teaching. 
No exams, and tests weren't too hard (all multiple choice q's), but you pretty much have to memorize everything. That being said pretty much everything you need to know is on the lecture slides.
Definitely recommend this course.

Tough grader, don't take her classes for an easy A. You will need to HARD CORE MEMORIZE tiny details that seem irrelevant. She is super nice and has wide open office hours and will clarify concepts for you if you ask.

Chill professor. Class only has exams. Homework is easy if you go to lectures. Really interesting course if you love dinosaurs. However, there is a lot of memorization in this course (basically you have to memorize every point of each slide). She does give lots of extra credit though. No textbook required either. Just go to lecture and memorize.
She's lovely and the class is laid out well, but the amount of memory is frankly, absurd. There is no other way to demonstrate knowledge, except through pure memorization.

Cindy is a pretty nice professor. In my opinion her exams were fair, but it requires a LOT of memorization. You need to be able to remember almost all details from her slides. If you don't have a strong memory, do not take the class! This class seems a bit too much for a 100 level course. Lots of people failed her exams.

cindy was such a nice prof and super willing to help her students. i would recommend taking this as an elective! the unit tests were pretty memorizing heavy and you had to know alot of material but it was overall very straightforward :)

It's hard, but should be expected when taking a course in university, even if it is an 100-level course. Heavy on memorization, so if that's not your thing, this course may not be the best for you. However, the course is super interesting. No paper, gives bonuses to improve your mark, and provides resources to use. She WANTS you to do well.

Cindy is a fun prof, the course material is interesting. Her grading is extremely critical, I would not remotely recommend this class to anyone who has the slightest issue with memorization. offers plenty of bonus marks though. Just found myself getting frustrated over how poorly I was doing on these exams compared to my other science classes.
Very kind professor that gives you a lot of bonus points. Also removes questions if class average is low. Would recommend to future students looking for a breadth science class to take.

Cindy is very nice and her lectures are fun. The only issue is how it's become nearly Impossible to do good on her tests. You can attend every single lecture, spend weeks studying and you will still get 60%. I've had an easier time with math and chem classes. way too hard for a first year class I would not recommend unless you want to cry.
Really helps with review of learning and is a very clear instructor, very easy to follow.

Cindy was an involved and thorough professor. She was super organized and took time to remember our names! She was a super approachable prof and had great lessons. Tests were super hard however and lengthy. However, I appreciate her opportunities to get bonus marks, see our tests, and boost our marks. Great prof!!!

Cindy was engaging and lively, and her passion and dedication brought out the best of the material. Tests were memorization-heavy, but you're provided with a plethora of diagrams, tools, and examples to assist with that. Honestly if you have the time to spare, why not take the dinosaur class?

Cindy is a nice prof but this class is not it. The tests are fair but it is pure memorization. You need to memorize every single small detail from the slides, so if you don't have a strong memory DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. This class is too hard for a 100-level elective.
Would probably take again

This class has interesting content but the content on the exams is pure memorization! Every tiny detail on the lecture slides- even things she mumbles in the back will be on the exam. First test, 45% of the class failed. The content on the exams are so pick and choose as well, you could study everything and she'll choose the least useful content.

I had a great experience with Cindy last fall! Before class ended we had to do a short assignment in groups which was fairly easy (participation marks) and there was a lot of homework, but it was super easy and could be done in 5 minutes. Tests were super easy and fair, just memorize the material.

Took this course in Fall 2023. The lectures were straightforward and didn't use the textbook (don't buy the textbook you won't need it). There are 4 labs and 4 tests which make up a majority of your grade plus daily worksheets which sounds like a lot of work (but keep in mind this class has no tutorial).
As long as you study, you should do fine.

I took this course as an elective and I thought it was very reasonable. Cindy is a very sweet and kind person. Make sure you really know the content because although the tests are very fair, they're very memory heavy

Textbook was good to have but the reading was recommended and not tested. There were a lot of assignments but they weren't too strenuous. The testing style was not a good fit for me. 50 minutes for a 50-60 mark exam, so I was in a blind panic. You really have to know the material to pass. Lecture was informative and I learned some solid stuff.

I took this course as a GPA booster elective and it was the biggest mistake Ive made, I probably scored better in my 4 credit stats course. Cindy is okay but the course has 4 extremely hard and time constraint exams which account for 80%. If you dont want to spend studying hours on end for a 101 course, stay clear of her and this course.

Cindy is fine and as well is the course but her tests are too long for the mere 50 minutes given to us. 1 Lab and 2 quizzes every week with 4 tough and extremely time constrained tests accounting for 20% each. Grading is also tough. Took it as an elective for an easy grade, ended up crying. Not recommended.
Online courses is awful. The test has really limited time to do. It is impossible to do it within 50 mins (30 MCQs + 15 short Qs). Quizzes and lab every week....
Test is extremely long for short period of time . Tough grader. Lab exercises is ok but they only account for 10% with 3 very long test, each account for 30%

Cindy has such a boring and slow voice it makes you fall asleep and retain no info. I took this class online and she is good at explaining things if you can pay attention. 90% of your grade is tests and 10% labs which take 3+ hours. The tests are long (30 MC and 15 short answer) and are impossible to get a good grade based off her marking.

I didn't find the course very hard but the average was really low for an intro class. Cindy was good, in office hours was pretty helpful as well. She cared about the students. But the tests left everyone a little rushed for time. Overall a solid course and very good prof as long as you do your part.

Cindy's tests are really fair, she explains everything about her lectures and nothing on the tests are outside of the lectures. So, if you annotate, STUDY and listen, then you will do well in the class. 50 mins for her tests are fair because you can get through the mc pretty quickly and have enough time to do the short answers.

This was the total opposite experience on what I was expecting for a first level course to dinos. For the most part, instead of testing us on info she emphasized in lectures like the key features of the dinos, she tested us on unimportant small details which made no sense to even test our learning on. These exams were a race against time. Um, no.

The tests are only 50 mins long and have 30 multiple choice and 15 short answer questions. I couldnt even finish the first 2 exams because of the time limit without even getting the chance to read some short answer questions. Only take this class if you have a photographic memory or already know A LOT about dinosaurs.

This class was delivered online through pre-recorded lectures. If you're okay with memorizing every small detail for a 50 minute exam, where you are expected to write very detailed written answers (grading is very tough) and multiple choice questions, this class will be for you.

I'm not sure how this class is in person, but avoid it if you are taking it online. The tests are extremely hard because you have very little time and they are all "applied knowledge" questions. Lectures were pretty boring and confusing because her explanations are super wordy. Would not recommend

103 went great, so I decided to take 101 with Cindy. It was a mistake. The exams had too many questions to finish within 50 mins. If you don't know the answer to a question as soon as you read it, consider it a lost mark. Assignment marking is harsh and nit-picky. Heed other comments and don't take this course unless you absolutely need it.

I took this for a science credit and it was a mistake. This class seems straight forward but is difficult. In the 50 minute time slots for class, tests that have 35-45 questions are given; it is extremely stressful, sometimes you cant even finish the test. Unless you are super good at memorizing a ton of info, I would not recommend this class.

Dr. Hansen puts a lot care and effort into her teaching. All the material is laid out well and easy to understand. Make sure to take detailed notes during lectures, and if you put some effort into studying it is not hard to do well. Tests are very fair, though she is a tough marker on the few written answer questions. Accessible and great Prof!

Do not take EASC 103 with her. As an elective course, she expects a lot from students during her exams. You have to memorize everything to do well on the exams. Her lectures are really annoying as she is v/ wordy when explaining her slides. As well, she keeps repeating her points. Her class is such a waste of time.

I took it when all courses got switched to online halfway through due to Covid-19 and it got so much easier online. The first half of the course was hard and the lab exam and midterm were also very hard. Make sure you know all the properties of every single mineral and rock.
Im highly recommend this course as b-sci. You literally just memorize the key point in the ppt, and no finals but three tests throughout the whole semester.

Cindy is an amazing prof! Her lectures are always fun and interesting, and I would definitely recommend taking EASC103, whether it's because you need a science credit or you just like dinosaurs. Of course you will need to study to do well (memorize the time periods the dinosaurs were around in!), but the tests are fair and Cindy is awesome!

Cindy is such a lovely person and more importantly, an AMAZING prof! She really knows how to teach people in a way that makes the information easy to understand, and sticks in your head. Her tests are fair, and test only whats on her slides, and she really emphasizes the points that she tests. Put in some effort, and you'll get a decent mark

I totally love this class. It is so interesting and so much fun because of Cindy. She's caring and so sweet and does everything in her power to make lecture fun. She brings dinosaurs and does fun labs for you to learn. Shes awesome!

This was an amazing class! I took it to fulfill my science credit and I was surprised by how fun it was. Lectures and slides are laid out very clearly which makes studying easy. Her labs and in-class activities are participation, not answer graded. This is a memorization class, but if you study early it's easy. She brings little dino toys to class.

This class is all about memorization and honestly if you go to most of the lectures and the labs, there are participation exercises every class to get easy points on, youll be fine. I honestly didnt study that great for tests and still got Bs. I also missed a few labs and lectures and ended up with a good grade. If you study an A is achievable.

Cindy Hansen is the best professor I have ever had and also best class I have ever taken!! She is the sweetest lady in the world and cares so deeply about her students and dinosaurs! Her tests are fair and as long as you study you should achieve a high mark! I also love the Dinos in the lab component she adds to this course!! They are super fun :)

Definitely my favourite professor ever. She really knows her stuff and it's clear that she has a genuine interest in the subject matter and wants to make it as interesting as possible for the students. The dinosaur unit was particularly fantastic as she brought in numerous samples (as she did for most lectures).

This would be an easy class if you were good at memorization. The exams are not open book, but thankfully, the questions are mostly multiple choices, fill-in-the-blanks, connect-the-dots kind of questions. She is a kind person, and lectures are fun to hear since she is passionate about dinosaurs. You may learn new and interesting things from her.

she is a picky examiner. She bases your answers off of everyone else's, and not just whether your answer is somewhat right. So, write your answers very detailed! Attend every class for extra notes. Her slides are easy to understand, but there is  A LOT to memorize!

I took this class to fulfill my final elective requirement. Amongst all of the 100-200 level electives that I have taken I found this to be by far the most difficult. There is no analytical thinking involved, but you better be good at memorizing great swaths amount of information. I just wish Cindy would curve, as her grading is really strict.

This class is NOT a GPA Booster. Do not get fooled by her saying "I don't expect much as this class is not a required easc course." If you are looking for easy B-Sci course take something else. Her lecture is easy, but exams were very difficult. You need to work hard and memorize everything single details on her lecture slides. No textbook.

For a first year class, it is very difficult, and not only that the professor is very strict, she actually wouldn't accept my doctors note, because it would be an inconvienance for her, because she would have to give the exam again, her lectures are easy, but her exams are not
Very nice and lectures are easy to follow. Everything you need to know are in the notes so the text is not necessary.
Cindy is alright but so much of what you learn in this class is memorization and she's happy to ding you where you're not spitting off the lecture slides.
She is passionate about the class but does a terrible job preparing you for the exams. I don't recommend taking this class.
110% helpful and friendly. No matter what, she'll do her absolute best to make sure you pass. I've never known a teacher who genuinely cares about her students as much. Don't let her down :)

She has been one of my favorite profs. She is very clear in her lectures and her expectations. I didn't do any readings, but the lectures and lecture notes (not posted online) and extremely helpful. The labs aren't too hard but you need to study a loooot for lab midterms. I highly recommend taking this with her if you put in the work.

Most amazing and dedicated teacher I've met. Willing to do anything and everything to make sure her students learn, and not just for the course. She's incredibly nice and genuine, and if you actually give a crap about your studies (compared to wanting easy credits or wanting to pass), she will be one of the best teachers you ever have.
Cindy was an great lecturer. I never skipped a class. However, her midterms and final were next to impossible, although the final was a bit easier. The lab helped my grade a lot.

Omg, I read the things here thinking she would be good. She is completely the opposite for academics at least. Sure she is nice and friendly as well as passionate and helpful. But my lord her tests and especially final were so bad 30/85 failed and she didn't even curve it. AVOID her does not prep you for final and tells you the wrong test content!
she makes the course very hard and she expects too much from students and its a 100 level course. NEVER TAKING ANY COURSE WITH HER.

She expects you to know A LOT. Memorize EVERYTHING for the first midterm. Her mineral and rock lab exam was really hard, there was a TON to memorize as well. Second lab exam is way easier. Final tbd... I am expecting the worst considering how hight of expectations she has for a first year course... :(

Yes this class was a lot of work and difficult, but if you put in a lot of effort you can get an A for sure! She was really nice and always explained things if you didn't understand. Tests were more on the difficult side, so be sure to study very hard! This class made me love earth science and look forward to lectures! Recommended!

ok whoever said shes hot needs their eyes checked! she looks like a 40 yr old lady =P she is nice but she barley took part or was around during labs. Her exams were moderately hard and lectures were dry as heck. Not a bad prof, but expect A LOT of work needed for this class there and at home!
Fantastic lab instructor!!  Cindy was patient and helped out so much.  Her help made the labs so much less stressful.  Cindy's explanations were very clear and helped sooo much for exam!!

I absolutely adore Cindy! She was an awesome lab instructor. The labs were somewhat difficult and you need to study A LOT for the lab midterm/final. She was extremely helpful and had a good sense of humour! She was also pregnant while teaching in the Spring 2009 semester! Congrats Cindy (if you ever read this) xD

Excellent lab instructor. Very clear instructions during lab and very willing to help. Easily approachable and a very lenient marker. Lab midterm and final were very fair. If you put the effort into this, it will definitely reflect on your mark. =)

A great lab instructor! Really makes the material very clear & explains things carefully. Offers TONS of office/lab time so you can study & catch up. A very fair marker & a good person, she cracks jokes in lab and will chat with you too. Definitely one of the best I've had in many years of school! I will take a course with her again in a flash.

Cindy's the lab instructor, but she's taught this course before apparently. Wow, she's by far the best instructor I've had through 3 yrs of school. She's sooo organized, crystal clear in her expectations and instruction. She's also very friendly and obviously loves what she does. I would definetly take a course with her again.
